items.title: Metocean Dashboard Briefing: Shortening Decision Loops Offshore
items.excerpt: A compact metocean workflow can turn raw forecast layers into a briefing that offshore teams can act on before the next shift starts.
items.body_markdown:
## Why it matters

Operational teams rarely need another raw weather dashboard. They need a clear read on how wind, waves, and current thresholds affect the next decision window.

## What changes in practice

A briefing-oriented workflow reduces the amount of interpretation required in the field. Forecast layers are translated into a sequence of go, hold, and prepare signals tied to the actual marine activity.

## What this dummy article is for

This seeded record exists to exercise the upcoming `/insight` listing logic and the future `/insight/[slug]` template with a realistic article shape.
items.seo_title: Metocean Dashboard Briefing for Offshore Decisions
items.seo_description: Dummy insight article for BW covering metocean dashboard briefings and operational decision support.

items.title: From Sensor to Shift Plan: Building a Practical Forecast Workflow
items.excerpt: Forecasting becomes more valuable when site observations, threshold logic, and shift planning are stitched into one repeatable workflow.
items.body_markdown:
## A workflow, not just a feed

Teams do not operate on data alone. They operate on timing, staffing, and risk tolerance. That means a useful forecast product must connect live observations to the specific decisions a site supervisor is making.

## Where dummy content helps

This placeholder article gives the CMS a second Products item so the listing page can render both feature and list states from the same category.

## Future use

When real editorial content is ready, the markdown body can be replaced without changing the collection shape.
items.seo_title: Forecast Workflow from Sensor to Shift Plan
items.seo_description: Dummy insight article for BW about turning observations and forecast data into a practical site workflow.

items.title: Port Access Window Review for a Coastal Construction Campaign
items.excerpt: A project recap showing how weather windows, tide timing, and logistics planning can be reviewed together before coastal work begins.
items.body_markdown:
## Planning the access window

For coastal construction, arrival timing and sea state cannot be treated separately. Access planning works best when marine conditions are reviewed together with vessel readiness and cargo sequence.

## Why this category matters

The Projects category should feel operational and concrete. This dummy article is written to test that tone in the future listing and detail pages.

## Reusability

The same collection can support project recaps, field notes, and implementation stories without changing the core schema.
items.seo_title: Port Access Window Review for Coastal Construction
items.seo_description: Dummy insight article for BW covering coastal project access planning and operational review.

items.title: Slope Stability Monitoring Rollout for a Remote Land Corridor
items.excerpt: Monitoring programs become more useful when geospatial readings, rainfall context, and response thresholds are presented as one operational picture.
items.body_markdown:
## Operational framing

Slope monitoring is only half technical. The other half is communication: which signal matters, who receives it, and what action follows.

## Why it is seeded now

This gives the Projects category a second article so the site can test both feature cards and list cards from CMS data later.

## Long-form shape

Markdown is used here because it keeps the body portable and easier to render consistently in Astro.
items.seo_title: Slope Stability Monitoring for Remote Corridors
items.seo_description: Dummy insight article for BW about slope monitoring workflows and operational response planning.

items.title: BW Opens a New Delivery Track for Rapid Forecast Briefings
items.excerpt: A short company update on packaging forecast outputs into a faster delivery format for project teams that need briefing-ready updates.
items.body_markdown:
## A tighter delivery loop

Editorially, company news should feel more concise than technical explainers. This dummy article tests that shorter, announcement-style content can still live in the same collection.

## What changed

The story frame is simple: a new delivery rhythm, clearer outputs, and easier handoff to field users.

## Why seed it

This record makes the Company News section immediately usable when the client-side listing fetch is implemented.
items.seo_description: Dummy company news article for BW describing a faster briefing delivery workflow.

items.title: Why Downscaling Matters for Site-Specific Forecast Confidence
items.excerpt: Broad regional models are useful, but site-specific operational decisions usually depend on how well forecasts are translated to local conditions.
items.body_markdown:
## From regional to local

Forecast confidence changes when terrain, coastline geometry, or surface roughness introduce local effects that broader models smooth over.

## Why this belongs in science and technology

This category should support explainers that are technical, but still readable by non-specialist decision makers.

## Editorial fit

The markdown body gives enough structure for headings and paragraphs without pulling in a heavy HTML editor.
items.seo_description: Dummy science and technology article for BW about downscaling forecasts to local operating conditions.

items.title: Turning Geospatial Layers into Solar Prospecting Decisions
items.excerpt: Solar screening improves when irradiation, terrain, access, and land constraints are evaluated together instead of in isolated reports.
items.body_markdown:
## A layered decision process

Promising solar sites do not emerge from irradiation alone. Teams also need to understand terrain, access routes, nearby infrastructure, and practical development constraints.

## Why this dummy exists

This second Science and Technology article completes the four-category seed set requested for the `/insight` listing page.

## What comes later

Once `bw-insights` is wired into the frontend, these placeholders can be swapped out incrementally with real editorial content.
items.seo_description: Dummy science and technology article for BW about geospatial screening for solar project decisions.
